basics.name: namePrefix
basics.label: Resource name prefix
basics.toolTip: Prefix for all resource names. 3-15 characters, lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. Must start with a letter. Example: gib-contoso-prod

constraints.validationMessage: 3-15 characters, lowercase letters/numbers/hyphens only. Must start with a letter.

subLabel.preValidation: Select the OS images to build

options.text: Select which golden images to build. At least one must be enabled. Each enabled image creates a separate Azure Image Builder template and image definition in the Compute Gallery.

elements.name: enableWindowsServer2022
elements.label: Windows Server 2022 (Datacenter Azure Edition)
elements.toolTip: Builds a golden image for Windows Server 2022. Suitable for IaaS VM workloads.

elements.name: enableWindowsServer2025
elements.label: Windows Server 2025 (Datacenter Azure Edition)
elements.toolTip: Builds a golden image for Windows Server 2025. Suitable for IaaS VM workloads.

elements.name: enableWindows11MultiSession
elements.label: Windows 11 Multi-Session (AVD pooled hosts)
elements.toolTip: Builds a golden image for AVD pooled session hosts (win11-24h2-avd SKU).

elements.name: enableWindows11SingleSession
elements.label: Windows 11 Single-Session (AVD personal / standard VMs)
elements.toolTip: Builds a golden image for AVD personal hosts or standard VMs (win11-24h2-ent SKU).

options.text: Windows Updates are always applied. All other customizations are optional.

elements.name: installAzureMonitorAgent
elements.label: Install Azure Monitor Agent
elements.toolTip: Pre-installs the Azure Monitor Agent during the build. Recommended for VM observability.

elements.name: installDefenderForEndpoint
elements.label: Configure Microsoft Defender for Endpoint prerequisites
elements.toolTip: Ensures Defender is enabled and signatures are up to date. Full MDE onboarding requires an org-specific package applied post-deployment.

elements.name: enableSecurityHardening
elements.label: Apply CIS-aligned security hardening
elements.toolTip: Applies registry hardening, disables legacy TLS/SMBv1, enables firewall, configures NTLM settings. Disables Print Spooler — re-enable if printing is required.

elements.name: enableAvdOptimizations
elements.label: Apply AVD optimizations (applies to Win11 images only)
elements.toolTip: Installs FSLogix, configures Teams for AVD, disables non-essential services and scheduled tasks, applies performance settings.

options.text: Builds run monthly on the selected day at the selected hour (UTC). Day 15 is ~3 days after Patch Tuesday — recommended for server images. A Logic App is deployed with an HTTP trigger for on-demand builds.

elements.name: buildScheduleDayOfMonth
elements.label: Build day of month
elements.toolTip: Day of month (1-28) the scheduled build runs. Day 15 = approximately 3 days after Patch Tuesday.

elements.name: buildScheduleHour
elements.label: Build hour (UTC)
elements.toolTip: Hour (0-23, UTC) the build starts. 2 = 02:00 UTC.

elements.name: notificationEmail
elements.label: Notification email address
elements.toolTip: Email address to notify on build success or failure via Azure Monitor alerts. Leave empty to disable.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid email address or leave empty.

elements.name: enableLogAnalytics
elements.label: Deploy Log Analytics workspace for build monitoring
elements.toolTip: Deploys a Log Analytics workspace (PerGB2018, 30-day retention) for centralized build log aggregation.

options.text: By default, AIB build VMs run in a Microsoft-managed network with outbound internet access. Enable VNet injection if your scripts are in private storage, or if corporate policy requires build VMs in your network.

elements.name: useVNetInjection
elements.label: Inject AIB build VM into a customer VNet
elements.toolTip: Routes the AIB build VM through your VNet. Required for private script storage or restricted outbound internet.

toolTip.virtualNetwork: VNet to inject the AIB build VM into.
toolTip.subnets: Subnet for the AIB build VM. Must have no NSG rules blocking outbound to Azure services.

elements.name: usePrivateScriptStorage
elements.label: Use private Azure Blob storage for customization scripts
elements.toolTip: Deploys a storage account and uploads scripts there. The AIB UAMI is granted Storage Blob Data Reader. Use when the build VM cannot reach GitHub.
